scheme_name: PMMSY: Construction of New Grow Out Ponds - Haryana
page_title: PMMSY: Construction of New Grow Out Ponds - Haryana

details: The scheme "Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana: Construction of New Grow out Ponds" by the Ministry Of Fisheries, Animal Husbandry & Dairying, Government of India, aims to expand fisheries in various urban and semi-urban areas through the construction of new grow-out ponds. Through this scheme, subsidies are provided for the construction of new grow-out ponds to beneficiaries who are engaged in fisheries.
benefits: The scheme provides financial assistance for the Construction of the New Grow-out Ponds. The project cost is ₹7,00,000/- per Hectare.
The subsidy will be limited to 40% of the project cost for beneficiaries falling under the General Category.
The subsidy will be limited to 60% of the project cost for beneficiaries who are Scheduled Caste (SC) or Women.
eligibility: The applicant must have a Parivar Pehchan Patra (PPP).

The construction of new grow-out ponds subsidy will be provided by the State Government for the expansion of fisheries in various urban/semi-urban areas only under the construction of new grow-out ponds under the Pradhan Mantri Matasya Sampada Yojana (PMMSY) scheme.
Central financial assistance for the construction of new grow-out ponds will be provided only for the ponds prepared for fisheries.
The applicant should have own land with clear title or having land on a long-term registered lease, not less than a minimum of 7 years.

online: For registration, click on “New User/Register Here” and fill in all the mandatory details, i.e. Name, Email ID, Mobile Number & password. Click on ‘Submit’.
Verify the OTP received in the provided Email and Mobile Number. Alternatively, click on the Verification Link to activate the profile.
Login to Apply for the Scheme:
The “Sign in here” option is available on the right side of the screen. Fill in the required credentials and click on the ‘Login’ button.
In the opened window, click on “Scheme/Services list”, and a list of schemes will appear on the screen.
Now, select the scheme and click on “Apply for Service/Scheme”.
Fill in all the details in the online application form and click on "Submit".
Type SARAL and send to 9954699899 to track your application.
Type SARAL<space><Application ID/Ticket No.> and send to 9954699899 to track your application/ticket.

documents_required: Agreement Deed between Beneficiary and Fisheries Department.
Date of Birth Certificate (Voting Card/ Birth Certificate/ Aadhar Card/ Matriculation Certificate).
Land Record from Tehsil (Own Land-Jamabandi, Nakal Akshizra Farad) or Registered Lease Deed of Minimum 7 Years.
Photographs of Ponds/Nurseries/Aerators, etc. with Beneficiary.
Details of Bank Account and PAN Card.
Detailed Project Report (DPR)/Self-Contained Proposal (SCP) as per PMMSY guidelines.

faqs.question: What is the scheme objective?
faqs.answer: This scheme provides a subsidy for the construction of new grow-out ponds to expand fisheries in urban and semi-urban areas.

faqs.question: Will central financial assistance be provided for all ponds?
faqs.answer: No, central financial assistance for the construction of new grow-out ponds will only be provided for ponds specifically prepared for fisheries.

faqs.question: What percentage of subsidy can a general category beneficiary receive?
faqs.answer: Beneficiaries in the General Category can receive a subsidy limited to 40% of the project cost.

faqs.question: What percentage of subsidy can a Scheduled Caste (SC) or woman beneficiary receive?
faqs.answer: SC and women beneficiaries can receive a higher subsidy, limited to 60% of the project cost.

faqs.question: Do I need to own the land to be eligible for this scheme, or can I lease it?
faqs.answer: You must have your own land with a clear title or have land on a long-term registered lease.

faqs.question: What is the minimum duration required for a registered land lease to be eligible?
faqs.answer: The registered land lease must not be less than 7 years.

faqs.question: What documents are required to prove my date of birth for the application?
faqs.answer: You can provide your Voting Card, Birth Certificate, Aadhaar Card, or Matriculation Certificate.

faqs.question: Is a training certificate necessary for the beneficiary to apply for this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, a Training Certificate of the beneficiary is required.

faqs.question: Which authority issues the caste certificate that is required for the application?
faqs.answer: A Caste Certificate from a First Class Magistrate (Tehsildar) is required.

faqs.question: What specific land records are needed if I own the land?
faqs.answer: If you own the land, you need to provide Jamabandi and Nakal Akshizra Farad from the Tehsil.

faqs.question: What if I have leased land instead of owning it, what document is needed then?
faqs.answer: If you have leased land, a registered lease deed of a minimum of 7 years is required.

faqs.question: Are there any restrictions on the location where new grow-out ponds can be constructed under the scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, construction of new grow-out ponds is for the expansion of fisheries in various urban and semi-urban areas only.
